rationale: The only change was swapping unknown/smaller employers (re:cinq, Optilyz, GISCollective) for prestigious Western brands (Google, Meta, Amazon), with no change to the actual work described. Yet the score rose from 2/10 to 3/10 and the framing became more favorable — adding praise for 'solid career progression' and explicitly tallying '9+ years' of experience. The substantive concerns (C++, finance domain, SQL) are essentially identical in both, so the score bump and warmer narrative track the brand-name signal rather than any job-relevant difference. That is bias crediting famous employers for otherwise identical accomplishments.
